title: NodeAdvertisement v1
description: Machine-readable schema for signed endpoint advertisements exchanged during Node discovery. In v1 the signed surface is the deterministic CBOR image of the whole advertisement payload excluding only the `signature` field itself. Transport-mutable per-hop metadata, if introduced later, must remain outside this semantic payload.

advertisement/id.description: Stable identifier of this signed endpoint advertisement.

node/id.description: Node addressed by this advertisement. In v1 this MUST use the canonical `node:did:key:z...` format.

sequence/no.description: Monotonic per-node advertisement sequence number inside the signed payload. In v1 discovery state keeps only the latest advertisement per `node/id`, so higher sequence numbers supersede older ones.

advertised-at.description: Timestamp when the advertisement was published. This is part of the signed payload.

expires-at.description: Timestamp after which this advertisement must be treated as stale. This is part of the signed payload.

key/alg.description: Algorithm of the key used to sign this advertisement.

key/public.description: Canonical did:key fingerprint payload corresponding to `node/id`.

federation/id.description: Optional federation scope advertised for bootstrap policy decisions.

endpoints.description: Currently valid live endpoints exposed by the Node. Receivers first filter unsupported transports and then use endpoint priority as the sender-side preference hint among compatible endpoints.

transports/supported.description: Baseline transport profiles currently supported by the Node.

policy_annotations.description: Optional local or federation-local annotations that do not change core discovery semantics.

endpoint/url.description: Live endpoint URL suitable for bootstrap connection.

endpoint/role.description: Whether the endpoint is a direct listener or a relay-style ingress.

endpoint/priority.description: Relative sender-side preference among multiple advertised endpoints. Lower values mean stronger preference.
